potential_hallucinations.requirement: The system shall check if a person is an Indian citizen.
The system shall verify the details of an Indian citizen.
The system shall generate a unique health ID for registered Indian citizens.
The system shall activate an account upon successful login.
The system shall provide access to relevant authorities based on set permissions.
The system shall allow adding new files when permission is granted.
The system shall store all the information provided by users.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: If the person is not an Indian citizen, they cannot continue.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall prevent non-Indian citizens from proceeding further in the process.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall support after activation, the system shall allow users to either add old files or set permissions.
hallucinations.reason: The original text does not mention that these options are supported 'after activation'. It only mentions them as choices available after account activation.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall store added old files.
ambiguities.reason: It is unclear if the stored data refers to old files or new files, or both. The original text does not specify this.

prompt:
You are a strict requirements validator.
Compare rewritten requirements against the source text.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this schema:
{
  "missing_requirements": [
    {
      "evidence_quote": "string",
      "suggested_requirement": "The system shall ..."
    }
  ],
  "hallucinations": [
    {
      "rewritten_requirement": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "duplicates": [
    {
      "requirement_a": "string",
      "requirement_b": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "ambiguities": [
    {
      "rewritten_requirement": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "overall_assessment": "pass|needs_review"
}

Source text:
<<<
A person starts by providing their citizenship status.

If the person is not an Indian citizen, they cannot continue. If they are an Indian citizen, their details are checked.

Once the details are verified, the person registers using their ID and receives a unique health ID. Using this ID, they sign in to their account.

If the login is successful, the account is activated.

After activation, the person has two options. They can either add their old files or continue by setting permissions.

If they choose to add old files, those files are stored.

If they choose to set permissions, access is provided to the relevant authorities. After that, the person’s files may be accessed if permission is given. If permission is not given, nothing further happens.

When access is allowed, new files can be added, and everything is stored.

At the same time, the stored data can be accessed by the government to view details and prepare reports.

All the information is finally stored, and the process ends.
>>>

Rewritten requirements JSON:
{
  "rewritten_requirements": [
    "The system shall check if a person is an Indian citizen.",
    "The system shall verify the details of an Indian citizen.",
    "The system shall generate a unique health ID for registered Indian citizens.",
    "The system shall activate an account upon successful login.",
    "The system shall support after activation, the system shall allow users to either add old files or set permissions.",
    "The system shall store added old files.",
    "The system shall provide access to relevant authorities based on set permissions.",
    "The system shall allow adding new files when permission is granted.",
    "The system shall store all the information provided by users.",
    "The system shall allow the government to access stored data to view details and prepare reports."
  ]
}
